content: Maya Hassan is the primary contact at Northwind Logistics; she runs the dispatch-automation team and is based in Paris.
summary: Primary contact: Maya Hassan, dispatch-automation lead at Northwind Logistics (Paris).

content: Northwind Logistics is on the Team plan (annual billing), EU-Paris region. Billing email: billing@northwind-logistics.fr. VAT ID FR12345678901 added 2026-04-19 — invoices from May 2026 onward carry reverse-charge wording.
summary: Northwind: Team plan, annual, EU-Paris. Billing billing@northwind-logistics.fr. VAT FR12345678901 (added 2026-04-19).

content: Northwind's primary use case is dispatch-automation: a routing copilot with memory across drivers and shipments. Projected ingest volume is ~500k shipment-episodes per month.
summary: Use case: dispatch-automation routing copilot. Volume: ~500k episodes/month.

content: Northwind codebase is fully async Python (FastAPI + asyncpg) and runs mypy --strict. Always show AsyncStatewaveClient with explicit type hints; never default to sync or to JS/TS examples.
summary: Default to async Python with explicit types (AsyncStatewaveClient). No sync, no JS/TS examples.

content: Northwind webhook URLs follow the pattern hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/statewave/<event-type>. Use this pattern in any code suggestion, runbook, or example written for them.
summary: Webhook URL pattern: hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/statewave/<event-type>.

content: When Northwind reports SSO 'invalid signature' across all users, first check the SAML signing-cert expiry on their Okta IdP — the 2026-04-11 incident (#4821) was caused by an expired cert that auto-rotation didn't refresh. Resolution path: rotate cert in Okta, upload new public cert in Account → SSO. New sessions clear ~30m later.
summary: SSO 'invalid signature' across all Northwind users → check Okta SAML cert expiry first; rotate + re-upload (~30m clear).

content: Ticket #4821 (2026-04-11): SSO outage at Northwind, all users 'invalid signature'. Root cause: expired SAML signing cert in Okta, auto-rotation didn't fire. Resolution: customer rotated cert, uploaded new public cert. Time to resolution: 45m. SLA: not breached. No code change required.
summary: #4821 (2026-04-11) — SSO outage, expired Okta SAML cert. Resolved in 45m, no SLA breach.

content: Ticket #4937 (2026-04-22): /v1/context timing out at 30s for Northwind. Root cause: a 60k-memory fleet subject was beyond the pgvector index sweet spot. Resolution: customer split the fleet subject into per-region subjects, latency returned to baseline. SLA breached on first response (276 minutes vs 60-minute target) — service credit issued (one prorated month). Internal action: route Northwind high-severity tickets directly to EU on-call.
summary: #4937 (2026-04-22) — /v1/context 30s timeouts on a 60k-memory subject. Fixed by per-region split. First-response SLA breached, service credit issued.

content: Northwind high-severity tickets must page the EU on-call rotation directly. Established after #4937, where US-hours triage missed the ticket and produced a 4h+ first-response SLA breach. Do not let high-sev Northwind tickets fall through the default global queue.
summary: High-sev Northwind tickets → page EU on-call directly (post-#4937).

content: When a Northwind login complaint arrives, distinguish between SSO-wide failure (everyone affected → check Okta SAML cert per #4821) and per-group slowness (only one IdP group affected → session-cache pressure, distinct symptom). Don't conflate them; the 2026-04-18 'drivers' group slowness was unrelated to #4821.
summary: Login complaints: everyone affected = SSO/Okta cert; only one group = session-cache, not SSO.

content: Northwind has filed a feature request for per-event-type webhook filtering (logged 2026-04-20). Currently they receive every webhook event and filter downstream. Status: on roadmap, no committed date. If procurement makes it a blocker, escalate to product.
summary: Open feature request: per-event-type webhook filters (roadmap, no commitment).

content: Northwind backfill recipe: POST /v1/episodes/batch with 200 episodes per call, parallelized 4-wide. Set last_compiled_at on each batch so the compiler doesn't re-process old data. ~6 hours for their full 3M-event backfill. The runnable async Python script (mypy --strict clean) was sent 2026-04-25 and uses the hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/<event-type> webhook for progress.
summary: Backfill: 200 episodes/call, parallelized 4-wide, set last_compiled_at. ~6h for 3M events. Async Python script sent 2026-04-25.

content: Northwind switched STATEWAVE_COMPILER_TYPE from heuristic to llm on 2026-04-28 because the heuristic compiler was extracting only ~5 memories per 100 shipment-event episodes. After the switch, a one-time idempotent recompile was run on existing subjects.
summary: Compiler: llm (switched from heuristic on 2026-04-28; recompiled existing subjects).

content: Renewal scheduled for 2026-09-15 with expansion intent — Northwind plans to add their dock-ops team. Account-management to deliver an expansion quote and a quarterly SLA-performance summary (with #4937 + service credit explicitly shown) by 2026-07-15.
summary: Renewal 2026-09-15, expanding to dock-ops team. SLA summary + quote due 2026-07-15.

content: Maya reports an urgent issue with SAML SSO returning 'invalid signature' for all users.
summary: Maya raises a critical issue with SAML SSO at Northwind.

content: Maya reports sluggish login for some users, which may be related to a previous SSO issue.
summary: Maya notes slow login issues for some users, possibly linked to SSO.

content: Maya requests a feature to filter webhook deliveries by specific event types.
summary: Maya submits a feature request for filtering webhook deliveries.

content: Maya reports a timeout issue with /v1/context and confirms it is related to a high number of memories.
summary: Maya experiences timeouts with /v1/context due to high memory count.

content: Northwind's renewal intent and SLA performance summary were discussed, with a follow-up for specific metrics.
summary: Renewal and SLA performance metrics were discussed for Northwind.

content: A QBR was scheduled with Maya and Henri to discuss renewal, feature requests, and other updates.
summary: A quarterly business review was scheduled to cover multiple topics.

content: To backfill 6 months of historical shipment events into Statewave, use POST /v1/episodes/batch with up to 200 episodes per call, parallelized 4-wide.
summary: The procedure for backfilling historical shipment events into Statewave is outlined.

content: Triage for latency issues involves checking per-subject memory count and recommending splits if thresholds are approached.
summary: Triage process for latency issues includes memory count checks.

content: Maya runs the dispatch-automation team at Northwind Logistics in Paris.
summary: Maya is the head of the dispatch-automation team at Northwind Logistics.

content: Northwind Logistics signed up for the Team plan.
summary: Northwind Logistics is on the Team plan.

content: Northwind Logistics plans to use Statewave for routing copilot memory across drivers and shipments.
summary: Northwind Logistics intends to utilize Statewave for routing copilot memory.

content: Northwind Logistics has a strong preference for typed SDKs and runs mypy --strict everywhere.
summary: Northwind Logistics prefers typed SDKs and uses mypy --strict.

content: The Python SDK for Statewave ships full Pydantic models and type stubs.
summary: Statewave's Python SDK includes Pydantic models and type stubs.

content: 500k shipment-events per month is well within the Team plan's headroom.
summary: The Team plan can handle 500k shipment-events per month.

content: Statewave allows webhooks to be registered without path constraints.
summary: Webhooks in Statewave can be registered without path constraints.

content: Northwind's standard webhook pattern is hooks.northwind-logistics.fr/<service>/<event-type>.
summary: Northwind uses a specific pattern for their webhooks.

content: Northwind's codebase is fully async, using FastAPI and asyncpg.
summary: Northwind's codebase is built on FastAPI and asyncpg for async operations.

content: The average rotation time for a new SAML cert is approximately 30 minutes for new sessions.
summary: New SAML certs take about 30 minutes to clear for new sessions.

content: Invoices can be found under Account → Billing → Invoices in Statewave.
summary: Users can access invoices through the billing section of their account.

content: Northwind added VAT ID FR12345678901 for their next invoice.
summary: Northwind has registered their VAT ID for invoicing.

content: Northwind's high-severity tickets must page EU on-call directly.
summary: High-severity tickets for Northwind require direct paging of EU on-call.

content: The heuristic compilation method under-extracts from non-conversational episode shapes.
summary: Heuristic compilation may result in lower memory density for non-conversational data.

content: Switching to STATEWAVE_COMPILER_TYPE=llm typically results in ~2× density on operational data.
summary: Using llm for compilation can double the density of operational data.

content: Northwind plans to renew their contract on 2026-09-15 with an intent to expand seats for the dock-ops team.
summary: Northwind's renewal is set for September 2026 with plans to expand their team.

content: Northwind's procurement requested a breakdown of SLA performance over the last quarter.
summary: Procurement is interested in SLA performance metrics.

content: The backfill batch size is set at 200 episodes per call, with a parallelization of 4.
summary: Backfill batch size and parallelization settings were confirmed.

content: Rate limits for dispatch-automation are per-IP sliding-window, not per-API-key.
summary: Rate limits are determined by IP address rather than API key.

content: Northwind's edge proxy collapses to a single egress IP, sharing the 50 req/s budget among all drivers' shipment writes.
summary: Northwind's edge proxy uses a single IP for rate limiting.

content: Maya's procurement signed a standard processor addendum on 2026-04-29.
summary: A standard processor addendum was signed by Maya's procurement.

content: Northwind is evaluating Statewave for their cross-border customs-clearance team in 2027.
summary: Northwind is considering Statewave for a new use case in customs clearance.

content: Maya was promoted to Director of Dispatch Automation on 2026-04-25.
summary: Maya's promotion to Director of Dispatch Automation was confirmed.

content: Henri Laurent is the CTO and decision-maker for renewal/expansion alongside Maya.
summary: Henri Laurent is now a key decision-maker for Northwind's account.

content: Northwind reported elevated latency on the routing-fleet subject, with p95 climbing from 280ms to 1.4s.
summary: Northwind experienced increased latency on their routing-fleet subject.

content: Northwind shipped a per-region split on 2026-05-04, reducing p95 latency back to 290ms.
summary: A per-region split was implemented to address latency issues.

content: Statewave's snapshot format is stable across minor versions, ensuring backward compatibility.
summary: Snapshot format stability and backward compatibility were confirmed.

content: Northwind's health score improved to 92/100, with no escalations and expansion in the pipeline.
summary: Northwind's health score increased, indicating positive performance.

content: Ticket #4937's root cause was that the Northwind fleet subject crossed 60k memories — beyond the pgvector index sweet spot. The mitigation was to split the fleet subject into per-region sub-subjects so each stays under the 60k threshold.
summary: Ticket #4937 root cause: 60k-memory threshold.
